question_text: Iron has a density of 7,800 kg/m³ and cork has a density of 240 kg/m³. Which material is denser, and what does this tell us about how their particles are arranged?
correct_answer: Iron is denser; iron has particles packed more tightly together than cork
explanation: Iron's higher density means its particles are much more closely packed together compared to cork. A denser material has more mass squeezed into the same volume of space.
source_name: KS3 Physics Curriculum - Matter and Materials
